title: Shipyard Net Spend
query: SELECT COALESCE((SELECT SUM(ShipPrice) FROM ShipyardBuy WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate), 0) AS bought, COALESCE((SELECT SUM(ShipPrice) FROM ShipyardSell WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate), 0) AS sold

details.text: {bought} Cr spent acquiring ships vs. {sold} Cr recovered selling them during the report period. Net ship spend is the true cost of your fleet evolution — the gap between buy and sell totals shows how much you've permanently sunk into hulls you've moved on from, which is almost always significantly more than you'd expect.
details.help: ShipyardBuy ShipPrice vs. ShipyardSell ShipPrice. bought drives the title as the gross acquisition spend.

tagLines: Shipyard transaction reconciliation compiled. Fleet hull acquisition vs. disposal financials noted.
Ships depreciate. You paid market price for them and sold them for what you could get. The gap is real.
Net fleet spend is the honest number behind every new ship announcement. Someone paid the old ship's exit cost.
